contract.filename_rule: slugify(note.title) + '.md'; filesystem-safe on all platforms (e.g. 'Meeting Notes 6/10' → 'meeting-notes-6-10.md'). When the title slugifies to empty (empty/whitespace-only title), fall back to the note id: 'note-<id>.md'. 'Untitled Note' slugifies normally (untitled-note.md).
contract.file_body_source: removeH1FromContent(note.content) — the title-stripped markdown the read view renders; NOT the full note.content
contract.control: download IconButton with a download icon, matching the existing Delete IconButton pattern in NoteActions
contract.placement: read-view action toolbar, alongside Edit and Delete
contract.generation: pure client-side; file generated in the browser from already-loaded note data; no backend route, no fetch

assertions: Read view of a note renders a download control (an icon button with a download icon) in the action toolbar alongside Edit and Delete.
Activating the download control triggers a browser file download whose filename is the slugified note title plus '.md' (e.g. a note titled 'Meeting Notes 6/10' downloads as 'meeting-notes-6-10.md').
The downloaded file body equals removeH1FromContent(note.content) — the title-stripped markdown — and does NOT contain the H1 title line.
Downloading performs no network request — the file is built from the note data already loaded into the read view.
A note whose title slugifies to empty (e.g. whitespace-only) downloads as 'note-<id>.md' using the note's id.
oracle.setup: A logged-in user viewing a note in the read (non-editing) view, with the note already present in loader data.
oracle.tooling: Vitest + React Testing Library with render from test-utils/render; mock or assert on the browser download mechanism (anchor click / object URL).

test_tooling.tooling: Vitest + React Testing Library with render from test-utils/render; mock or assert on the browser download mechanism (anchor click / object URL).
